detail: init objective="Review GitHub PR #115 (fix(upstream): suppress Accept-Encoding to fix OpenRouter SSE streaming batching), determine from the current source and upstream protocol behavior whether it is correct and release-safe, and land the best production-grade form into opencodex. Scope includes the PR diff, directly related upstream request/header construction, focused regression tests for SSE behavior and header preservation, CI/release documentation or devlog evidence required by this repository, and branch/release operations needed to promote the accepted change through dev, preview, and main. Preserve all unrelated user and agent changes, especially the untracked .claude/ tree; do not bundle unrelated refactors, dependency upgrades, or existing PRs/issues. DONE means an independently audited patch is merged or equivalently integrated, targeted and full relevant tests/typecheck/privacy/build gates pass, real activation evidence proves upstream requests no longer negotiate compressed/batched SSE where intended without breaking explicit user headers, dev/preview/main are pushed in the repository's established order, CI is green, and both npm preview and stable releases plus GitHub releases are verified. NOOP means the PR is already fully present or demonstrably unnecessary, with evidence. BLOCKED means credentials, branch protection, unavailable upstreams, or CI infrastructure prevent completion after reasonable retries. UNSAFE means the proposed transport behavior would create a material compatibility or security regression. NEEDS_HUMAN means only a maintainer policy judgment can resolve a conflict. BUDGET_EXHAUSTED applies only if the unattended wall-clock exceeds 90 minutes or release retries exceed three per stage. Use existing GitHub/npm credentials only, make no paid external calls, keep write scope to the PR-related implementation/tests/devlog plus release version commits, and verify with gh PR/check inspection, focused tests, bun test --isolate tests, bun run typecheck, bun run privacy:scan, bun run build:gui when package release gates require it, and npm/GitHub registry evidence. First work-phase: inspect and audit PR #115 against the current dev tree and write a diff-level implementation/merge plan before changing code." criteria=0
